findings.message: New scrape job 'redis' targets 'redis-exporter:9121', but no 'redis-exporter' service is defined in any compose file. observability.yml (the overlay merged into dev/staging/prod) defines a postgres-exporter but no redis-exporter, and grep across infra/ finds 'redis-exporter' only in this scrape config. The scrape target has no corresponding exporter, so this job will permanently fail in every environment — observability that never resolves.
findings.suggestion: Add a redis-exporter service (e.g. oliver006/redis_exporter) to infra/compose/observability.yml alongside postgres-exporter, wired to the redis battery via REDIS_ADDR, so the target exists in prod by construction. If redis is not actually deployed, drop this scrape job instead.

findings.message: The new 'redis' scrape job introduces a redis metrics surface with no matching alert rule and no dashboard panel. infra/observability/prometheus/alerts/ has alertmanager/otel/postgres/prometheus/slo rule files but no redis_alerts.yml, and infra/observability/grafana/dashboards/ contains no redis dashboard. Scraped redis metrics would be collected but never alerted on or visualized — an incomplete observability surface.
findings.suggestion: Add infra/observability/prometheus/alerts/redis_alerts.yml (e.g. redis_up / connected_clients / used_memory thresholds, routed to an Alertmanager receiver) and a redis Grafana dashboard so the scraped metrics are both alertable and viewable.
